title: Candle Wax on Upholstery
timeEstimate: 30–60 minutes; freeze + lift only at home, NO iron
stainChemistry: Candle wax is paraffin (petroleum hydrocarbon) + sometimes fragrance + sometimes dye. Upholstery is mostly synthetic (polyester, microfiber, sometimes acetate / olefin). The classic iron-through-paper trick that works on COTTON FABRIC is DANGEROUS on synthetic upholstery — heat melts polyester / acetate / olefin permanently. Even on natural-fiber upholstery (cotton, linen blends), the iron + structured-furniture combination risks shape change. Universal-safe path on upholstery: freeze + lift mechanically + Goo Gone for residue + treat any colored-wax dye separately. NO iron on upholstery at home.
whyThisWorks: Cold makes paraffin brittle, lifts off mechanically without water or heat. Goo Gone (citrus solvent) on a cloth tamps residual wax film. Pro applies controlled solvent for stubborn residue. Code check determines if water-based steps allowed. The iron-through-paper trick is a COTTON FABRIC technique — does NOT transfer to upholstery.
defaultAssumption: Synthetic upholstery (most furniture). NO iron. Code tag determines water step. Zip-off washable cushion cover = wash separately.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Good light, ice in plastic bag
spottingProtocol.instruction: Find code tag (W / W-S / S / X). X = vacuum only, pro. Ice bag on wax 10–15 min until brittle. NEVER direct ice on upholstery.
Crack frozen wax with the edge of a plastic card. Lift off bulk pieces. Don't gouge upholstery — flat-edge lift only.
Apply Goo Gone to cloth (NEVER pour on upholstery). Tight blotting on residual wax film. Flip cloth as it picks up. Ventilate. Hidden-area test on dyed upholstery.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn + cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: W / W-S: Dawn damp cloth to clear Goo Gone residue. S-only: skip — dry buff with clean cloth.
If colored wax left dye: hidden-area test 3% peroxide. Damp cloth 5 min. Pigment is a separate stain after wax is gone.
Weighted dry towel 30 min. Fan-dry. Inspect in daylight.

steps: 2. Ice freeze 10–15 min. Crack and scrape bulk with flat-edge.
3. Goo Gone / POG on cloth, tight blotting.
4. Dawn damp cloth (W/W-S) or dry buff (S).
5. 3% H₂O₂ for residual colored-wax dye (hidden test).
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold (freeze) + ambient solvent — NO heat / NO iron
warnings: NEVER iron on upholstery — synthetic upholstery melts under iron
NEVER direct ice on upholstery — water damage
CODE CHECK — S-only / X change protocol
NEVER pour solvent on upholstery — flood = batting saturation
Pigment is a separate stain after wax is gone

homeSolutions.agent: DO NOT Iron Upholstery — That Trick Is for Cotton Fabric
homeSolutions.instruction: The classic "iron + brown paper bag" wax trick is for COTTON FABRIC. It is DANGEROUS on upholstery. Most upholstery is synthetic (polyester, microfiber, sometimes acetate or olefin) — heat from an iron MELTS those fibers permanently. Even on natural-fiber upholstery, the iron + structured-furniture combination risks shape change AND batting saturation. Universal-safe upholstery wax path: freeze + lift mechanically + Goo Gone for residue.

Find the upholstery tag (under cushion). W or W-S = full protocol. S-only = solvent only. X = vacuum only, pro. If the cushion cover zips off and is washable, take the cover off and machine-wash cold (with the wax already removed via freeze + lift). Far easier than treating in place.
Press a zip-lock bag of ice on the wax for 10–15 minutes. NEVER put ice directly on the upholstery — water damage. The wax gets hard and brittle.

homeSolutions.agent: Crack and Lift — Flat Edge of Plastic Card
homeSolutions.instruction: Once frozen, use the FLAT edge of a plastic card to crack and lift the wax pieces off. Don't scrape — flat-edge lift only. Most surface bulk comes off this way.

homeSolutions.agent: Goo Gone on a White Cloth — Never Poured
homeSolutions.instruction: Put Goo Gone (orange bottle, citrus solvent) on a clean white cloth. NEVER pour on upholstery. Press and tamp the residual wax film. Flip cloth as it picks up wax. Open windows — flammable. Hidden-area test on dyed upholstery first.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn or Dry Buff Depending on Code
homeSolutions.instruction: W / W-S code: Dawn in cool water on a damp cloth, press 5 min, blot. Clears solvent residue. S-only code: skip — dry buff with a clean cloth instead.

homeSolutions.agent: If Colored Wax — Treat the Dye Separately
homeSolutions.instruction: Once wax is gone, you might see the candle's pigment color (red, blue) still in the upholstery. That's now a dye stain. Hidden-area test 3% peroxide. Damp cloth 5 min on a tested-clean spot.
Stack a dry towel on the spot, weight with a book, 30 min. Fan-dry. Inspect in daylight.

steps: 1. NO IRON on upholstery — synthetic fabric melts.
2. Code tag check. X = pro. Zip-off cover = wash separately.
3. Ice freeze 10–15 min. Crack and lift bulk with flat-edge plastic card.
4. Goo Gone on cloth, tight blotting. Ventilate.
5. Dawn damp cloth (W/W-S) or dry buff (S).
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if upholstery is X-code, structured silk / wool / leather, or wax has been ironed in (likely melted into fiber).
warnings: NEVER IRON on upholstery — synthetic fabric melts
NEVER pour solvent or water on upholstery
Pigment is a separate stain after wax
Cushion cover wash is the easy path when available

fiberSensitivities: Synthetic upholstery (polyester, microfiber, olefin) melts under iron
Pigment is a separate stain after wax

materialWarnings: NEVER iron on upholstery — that's a cotton-fabric trick
Freeze + lift + Goo Gone is the safe upholstery path
Pigment is a separate stain after wax
Cushion cover wash is the easy path when available

escalation.when: X-code upholstery, silk / wool / leather upholstery, wax already ironed in (melted), residual colored-wax pigment after peroxide.
escalation.whatToTell: "Candle wax on [W / W-S / S / X / unknown] upholstery, [white / colored wax], age [fresh / aged / ironed-in], home-treated with freeze + flat-edge lift + Goo Gone + [Dawn / dry buff] + [3% peroxide on colored-wax dye]. NO iron used. [Residual wax / pigment / iron damage suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional upholstery cleaner with solvent spotting

contextQuestions: Upholstery code — W / W-S / S / X / unknown?
Fabric — polyester / microfiber / cotton / silk / leather?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Microfiber often contains acetate — acetone-sensitive. Olefin (Berber-style upholstery) softens under modest heat. Silk / wool / leather upholstery = pro from minute one. Pre-ironed wax = melted into fiber, pro.
